{"step_by_step_thinking": "Cognitive theory is a cognitive theory of learning, which focuses on mental processes such as memory, perception, and problem-solving. Hierarchy of needs is a motivational theory in psychology comprising a hierarchy of human needs, proposed and popularized by Abraham Maslow in the 1940s. Social learning theory is a theory of learning and behavior which proposes that new behaviors can be acquired by observation, imitation, and modeling. Classical conditioning is a behavioral theory that explains how behaviors can be learned through associations. Among these options, cognitive theory is not a behavioral theory as it focuses on mental processes rather than observable behaviors.", "answer_choice": "A"}
